chunks.text: Accessors Wiki | Obsolete / gdp module Recent Changes - Search : iCyPhy Home Accessors Home Accessors Wiki Editing Instructions All Recent Changes edit SideBar View Edit History Print Obsolete / gdp module The GDP is no longer supported by the Access

odule The GDP is no longer supported by the Accessors Framework. This page exists as a historical reference. https://gdp.cs.berkeley.edu/redmine/ describes the The Global Data Plane (GDP): "The GDP is a middleware that provides a data-centric glue f

s a middleware that provides a data-centric glue for swarm applications. The basic primitive is that of a secure single-writer append-only log stored on potentially untrusted distributed infrastructure. Logs natively support a publish-subscribe mode,

e. Logs natively support a publish-subscribe mode, making GDP a communication tool. At the same time, data in a GDP log can be stored for long-term in a fault-tolerant manner, thus making it an ideal tool for data-storage." To use the GDP Acessors, y

tool for data-storage." To use the GDP Acessors, you must have certain packages installed on your local machine. The best way to do that is to run the script: accessors/web/gdp/adm/gdp-setup.sh The idea is to use the GDP Java interface to insulate ac

ea is to use the GDP Java interface to insulate accessor development from changes to the GDP wire protocol. An alternative would be to write a GDP JavaScript Interface that uses the GDP wire protocol. Cape Code and the GDP $PTII/lib/gdp-X.Y-Z.jar -

. Cape Code and the GDP $PTII/lib/gdp-X.Y-Z.jar - The Java/GDP interface jar file, which is included in the classpath by running (cd $PTII; ./configure) GDP Java Interface -> Update Ptolemy $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp - The Cape Code Host

emy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp - The Cape Code Host implementation of the gdp accessor module. GDPHelper (JavaDoc) - Downloads and builds the gdp. Tests $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/test/GDPManager.tcl - Downloads and build the GDP, starts

Manager.tcl - Downloads and build the GDP, starts up the daemons and creates a few logs $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/test/ | $PTII/ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/test/auto - Ptolemy models that test the GDP. To run these tests, install

ls that test the GDP. To run these tests, install Ptolemy II , install the gdp libraries by running gdp/adm/gdp-setup.sh and then run $PTII/bin/capecode ptolemy/actor/lib/jjs/modules/gdp/test/auto/*.xml http://terra.eecs.berkeley.edu:8080/job/ptII/

xml http://terra.eecs.berkeley.edu:8080/job/ptII/ - The PtII Jenkins nightly build which runs the above tests GDPTestGenerator - a model that is deployed on terra that generates regular test data. Node.js and the GDP GDP JS GDP JS Callbacks GDP JS No
